label: PASS
rationale: LockOnReset contains reset type 0, and reset_types defines 0 as Power Cycle; on that reset the docs say ReadLocked and WriteLocked are set to True, so Get of WriteLocked=True is compliant.

label: FAIL
rationale: With LockOnReset=[0], a Power Cycle should set WriteLocked to True per 5.7.2.2.10 and reset_types value 0; returning WriteLocked=False is not compliant.

label: PASS
rationale: ReadLockEnabled is True and LockOnReset includes Power Cycle reset type 0, so the docs require ReadLocked to become True; Get of ReadLocked=True is compliant.

label: FAIL
rationale: LockOnReset=[0] matches the Power Cycle event, which should set ReadLocked and WriteLocked to True; returning ReadLocked=False is not compliant.

label: PASS
rationale: The docs state an empty LockOnReset set means ReadLocked and WriteLocked do not change on any reset; WriteLocked was set False and remained False after Power Cycle.

label: FAIL
rationale: For an empty LockOnReset set, the docs say WriteLocked does not change on any reset; it was set False before Power Cycle, so returning True is not compliant.

label: PASS
rationale: LockOnReset contains reset type 1, which reset_types defines as Hardware, not Power Cycle; the Power Cycle event should not set WriteLocked, so it remains False.

label: FAIL
rationale: LockOnReset=[1] identifies Hardware reset, while the trajectory performs Power Cycle; since the reset type does not match, WriteLocked should not change from False to True.
